profile.name: Sagar Koirala
profile.title: Embedded Systems & Firmware Design Engineer
profile.overview: Designing robust, low-latency, and high-integrity hardware architectures and embedded software solutions from concept to production.

philosophy.title: SIGNAL INTEGRITY AT THE TRACE LEVEL
philosophy.description: Silicon behavior changes outside the pristine lab environment. I design multi-layer PCB stack-ups and optimize high-speed hardware paths specifically to isolate signal corruption and transmission hazards before they reach the execution loop.

philosophy.title: STABILITY THROUGH EDGE STRESS TESTING
philosophy.description: System reliability cannot be proved in a vacuum. From tracing unstable power rails to mapping task routines in RTOS environments, every component layer must survive aggressive hardware bring-up, signal probing, and exhaustive logic-analyzer verification.

philosophy.title: BRIDGING HARDWARE WITH FIRMWARE LAYER
philosophy.description: System integration slows down when engineering disciplines live in isolation. I actively bridge the gap between schematic board designs and low-level code architecture, developing custom device drivers and testing scripts to keep deployment cycles moving.

projectsMeta.title: Atlas Telemetry Unit & CANbridge
projectsMeta.summary: 4-layer automotive STM32 telemetry system streaming real-time vehicle data over LTE and USB CDC.

projectsMeta.title: TelloESP32 Open-Source Library
projectsMeta.summary: Production-ready C++ Arduino library for UDP flight control and H.264 video streaming from dual-core ESP32.

bullets: Operated UHF/VHF ground station for BIRDS-3 CubeSat missions.

Developed LoRa-based ground sensor terminals for glacial lake monitoring, researching satellite-relayed (store-and-forward) telemetry via BIRDS-3 CubeSat; optimized RF link budgets, designed high-gain directional antennas, and integrated GSM telemetry fallbacks for rugged field deployment.

Publications: International Astronautical Federation : 9/21/2022 [https://iafastro.directory/iac/paper/id/72181/ext/appendix/IAC-22,E5,4,8,x72181.pdf] Satellite Ground Sensor Terminal Design: Use of LoRa to Transmit Remote Sensor Data from Nepal’s Himalayan Region for Disaster Mitigation

timeline.role: Bachelor of Engineering — Robotics and Automation Systems
timeline.company: King Mongkut's University of Technology North Bangkok (KMUTNB)
bullets: Sakura Science Program (Japan): Won 1st prize in a cross-cultural engineering competition.

timeline.company: International Institute of Space Technology for Economic Development
bullets: **Bootloader & OTA**: Designed a fault-tolerant Secondary Bootloader (IAP) for STM32 with a custom CAN protocol, Flash memory protection, and CRC verification for reliable over-the-air firmware updates.
**Firmware Design**: Developed a custom driver for a STspin3201 based Reaction wheel controller for CubeSats ADCS system.

**Board Bring-up**: STM32F1 based PCB design using Altium designer, for a custom Magnetic Torquer Testing rig, which included integration of Magnetometer (measuring the output magnetic field strength), current sensors, and motor drivers.
**Test Tooling**: Built a Python/Tkinter GUI (MTQ-Tester) for Magnetic Torquer hardware testing — multi-threaded real-time serial data visualization and live plotting.

Designed electronics hardware and software for a Sun-sensor calibration platform, a rotating platform with 2 degrees of freedom moving on azimuth and elevation axis. The system was driven by two stepper motors and was controlled by STM32F103 MCU with A4988 driver. Magnetic hall sensor was added for rotors feedback.
**100% Job Success Score** with 5-star ratings across all engagements.
Designed and delivered complex firmware, IoT, and embedded hardware architectures.
*Note: Detailed technical implementations for freelance engagements are cataloged in Section 4 below (see: Drone Telemetry, TelloESP32, and Narvar IoT).*

Developed CANsense — a C++/Qt tool for CAN bus analysis and reverse engineering: automated the manual process of recording CAN logs during drives and analyzing them in Excel by writing STM32 firmware for live capture and implementing real-time pattern-based highlighting to map changing signal IDs.
Modular software architecture with CLI backend and GUI frontend; **reduced vehicle signal decoding time by 70%**
Mentored primary and secondary school students in building, programming, and troubleshooting VEX IQ and V5 robotics kits.
Developed and delivered a comprehensive learning session on PID (Proportional-Integral-Derivative) control systems for the school's technology faculty and students.
Earned official certifications as a VEX IQ Scorekeeper and Head Referee.

testimonials.title: Embedded UI & Firmware Delivery
testimonials.text: Sagar was extremely professional, knowledgeable and communicative. He finished my project in due time and went above and beyond. I highly recommend him for your electronic programming needs!

testimonials.title: Drone Telemetry Integration
testimonials.text: The freelancer consistently completed tasks on time with excellent results. Outstanding skills, extensive experience, and exceptional support—highly recommended!
